notes: Shuttle XPC and similar compact desktops; later displaced by Mac mini, Intel NUC, laptops, and eventually smartphones/tablets
NetBurst microarchitecture CPU; high clock speeds 1.4-3.8 GHz; last of the Pentium brand before Core 2 Duo (2006)
Consumer-focused Windows XP variant optimized for living-room PCs with TV tuners and media playback; folded into Windows Vista/7 Home Premium
External peripheral bus standard; replaced legacy PS/2, serial, parallel, and SCSI ports; key enabler of small-form PCs by eliminating expansion-slot dependence
Consumer optical read/write drives; standard in mid-2000s PCs; phased out with laptop slim form factors
Style-first all-in-one PC design; Bondi Blue original 1998 launch redefined consumer PC aesthetics
